behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: this behavior can fail at the parser/range-resolver/function-evaluator handoff (e.g., `A1:A10` parsed correctly but resolved as `A1:A9`). Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI output dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is in-memory evaluation only (no disk, HTTP, subprocess).
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 values, evaluate formula text `=SUM(A1:A10)` through the real formula engine pipeline, assert exact numeric result.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Chosen test exercises module wiring; a pure SUM unit test would miss range-reference handoff bugs.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure appears at dependency-graph/evaluation-order handoff between referenced formula cells, not in one local function. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no agent chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only describes cross-cell computation in engine memory.
behaviors.test_description: In one workbook set A1 numeric, B1 `=A1*2`, C1 `=B1+1`; run recalculation and assert C1 reflects cascaded computation.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires multiple engine modules (reference resolution + evaluator + dependency tracking).

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: cycle detection must observe references across at least two cells via dependency graph construction and evaluator interaction. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI output dependency, no multi-agent flow. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not involve external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Create A1 `=B1+1` and B1 `=A1+1` in-memory, trigger evaluation, assert circular-reference error code/type and no hang.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A unit test of graph cycle algorithm alone could miss integration bug where parser emits wrong edges.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: exact-match behavior depends on handoff from cell-range extraction/type coercion into VLOOKUP evaluator; bugs often appear at that interface. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is spreadsheet function semantics only.
behaviors.test_description: Populate an in-memory lookup table range in cells, evaluate VLOOKUP formulas for found and missing keys, assert returned value and not-found error behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Testing only a standalone VLOOKUP helper could miss range addressing/type conversion defects.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Stayed at Unit: behavior text is primarily expression semantics (nested conditional evaluation) and can fail within one evaluator object/function without module handoff. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because no specific cross-module boundary is required by the behavior text beyond local expression evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Call expression evaluator (or equivalent single-object API) with controlled input values for A1 and nested IF expression; assert outputs `low`/`medium`/`high` for boundary cases.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Description can be satisfied with one local evaluator boundary and no I/O.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: Stayed at Unit: this is local function semantics (string joining + empty-value handling) and does not inherently require multi-module orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require dependency graph/scheduler/persistence boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Test CONCATENATE evaluator/function directly with argument lists including empty values and separators; assert output strings skip empties as specified.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A focused function/object test is sufficient to catch the real failure mode.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: failure occurs at sheet-name resolution/interface between parser tokenization and workbook sheet registry lookup. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only concerns in-memory workbook/sheet resolution.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set Sheet2!A1, evaluate `=Sheet2!A1+1` on Sheet1, assert resolved value.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs multiple modules (parser + workbook sheet resolver + evaluator).

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: real failure boundary is dependency tracker + scheduler + evaluator handoff during invalidation/recalc propagation. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: rejected System because recalculation order is internal engine behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain/diamond in-memory, mutate an upstream cell, trigger recalculation, assert final dependent values and that recomputation order respects topology (via observable hooks/logs/version counters).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single-function tests cannot validate propagation across module boundaries.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: undo correctness depends on command history/state snapshot handoff with formula engine recalculation/cache restoration. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: In-memory workbook: set formula with dependents, edit formula, verify changed results, perform undo, assert original formula text and dependent computed values are fully restored.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires orchestrating undo manager + recalculation engine, beyond one local function.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Unit->Integration proof: serialization/deserialization handoff across persistence modules is required. Integration->System proof: behavior explicitly requires real disk save/load in custom binary format; real filesystem writes/reads can expose truncation, flush, path, and binary I/O issues hidden by in-process fakes. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on AI/ML output.
behaviors.test_description: Write workbook with representative formulas and floating-point values to a real temp file, reload via normal load path, assert formula text equality and numeric equality within strict precision policy.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A mock filesystem could miss real binary I/O and file-level corruption edge cases required by this behavior.
